task_completion.description: Create the alpr_query directory in the Talon user folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file explorer, terminal, or IDE file tree showing the creation or existence of the folder 'alpr_query' inside 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\'.

task_completion.description: Implement the user.police_phonetic list in phonetic.talon-list
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'phonetic.talon-list' open in a text editor. It must contain a list definition for 'user.police_phonetic' and show mappings of APCO terms (e.g., adam: A, boy: B) and digits to their respective characters.

task_completion.description: Define the police_plate capture in alpr_query.py
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'alpr_query.py' open in a text editor. It must contain a Talon capture definition (e.g., @mod.capture) named 'police_plate' that takes the phonetic matches and concatenates them into a continuous string.

task_completion.description: Define the lookup_towed_vehicle action with the SQLite database query
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'alpr_query.py' in a text editor showing an action named 'lookup_towed_vehicle(plate: str)'. The function must contain code to connect to the SQLite database at 'C:\Users\Docker\talon_data\towed_vehicles.db' and query the 'towed' table.

task_completion.description: Implement the correct output strings using actions.insert()
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look inside the 'lookup_towed_vehicle' function in 'alpr_query.py'. It must use 'actions.insert()' to output either 'VEHICLE FOUND: [Color] [Make] at [Towed_to_Address]' or 'VEHICLE NOT FOUND IN TOW DATABASE' based on the query result.

task_completion.description: Define the voice command in alpr_query.talon
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'alpr_query.talon' open in a text editor. It must contain the voice command 'tow search <user.police_plate>:' and show it triggering the 'user.lookup_towed_vehicle()' action with the captured plate.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually created and saved the files rather than just printing code to the terminal
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the files being edited in a genuine text editor or IDE (like VS Code, Notepad, or Vim/Nano in the terminal) and saved to the disk.

integrity.description: Verify the agent implemented actual database query logic instead of hardcoding responses
integrity.visual_evidence: Check 'alpr_query.py' to ensure the agent uses a database library (like sqlite3) to execute a SELECT statement, rather than using 'if plate == ...' to fake the output.

integrity.description: Verify the agent split the code into the correct file types as instructed
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are three distinct files created: a '.talon-list' file, a '.py' file, and a '.talon' file, rather than dumping all Talon and Python code into a single file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target directory for the Talon module is exactly 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\alpr_query'. Note: The database file 'C:\Users\Docker\talon_data\towed_vehicles.db' might not exist in the environment due to a missing setup script, but the agent is still expected to write the code exactly as if it does exist.
